summary.intent: Fix GitHub issue #602 where GPT-5.x models were not recognized as supporting reasoning_effort, then improve the architecture to make capability detection more future-proof.
summary.outcome: The immediate fix was applied (gpt-5 prefix pattern added to supports_reasoning_for_model) and tests pass. A larger Option 2 refactor (adding capabilities metadata to ModelInfo/DiscoveredModel) was started but not completed — work was committed before a branch merge.

repo: supports_reasoning_for_model() is a standalone string-pattern function in crates/providers/src/lib.rs, unlike supports_tools() and supports_vision() which are trait methods on LlmProvider — reasoning was the odd one out.
ModelInfo has ~22 construction sites across the codebase, making struct field additions a non-trivial refactor.
The GraphQL types in crates/graphql/src/types/mod.rs already expose supports_tools and supports_vision but was missing supports_reasoning.
The model picker generates @reasoning-low/medium/high virtual variants based on supports_reasoning_for_model() output.
The worktree for this work is at /Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/historical-carrot

code.finding: Added `if id.starts_with("gpt-5") { return true; }` before the DeepSeek arm in supports_reasoning_for_model()
Test updated: moved gpt-5.2 to supports-reasoning assertions, added gpt-5.4, gpt-5.4-mini, gpt-5, gpt-5-mini
supports_reasoning_for_model() function — the central string-pattern allowlist that needs updating for every new model family

workflow: Worktrees are used at /Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/ for branch isolation.
Provider tests (261 total) are run with cargo test in the providers crate to validate changes.
A beads issue was created as part of the fix workflow.

friction: Had to commit in-progress work before merging main into the feature branch, indicating the merge was not planned ahead of time.
The Option 2 refactor (capabilities field on ModelInfo) required reading ~22 ModelInfo construction sites across many files, making it a large and tedious change.
The root cause of issue #602 was architectural — reasoning support was not co-located with the provider that owns that knowledge, making it easy to miss new model families.
open_items: Option 2 refactor (add capabilities field to ModelInfo/DiscoveredModel) was started but not completed — multiple files modified (crates/chat/src/lib.rs, crates/gateway/src/local_llm_setup.rs, crates/gateway/src/server.rs, crates/provider-setup/src/lib.rs, crates/providers/src/lib.rs) and committed, but the branch merge with main is pending.
GraphQL type for ModelInfo needs supports_reasoning field added (identified but not confirmed as done).
OpenAI /v1/models API is starting to expose capability metadata — could be leveraged to eliminate static pattern matching entirely for discovered models (noted as ideal end-state).
No CI test exists to cross-check the static model catalog against supports_reasoning_for_model() to catch future regressions when new model families are added.
